name: Blood Pressure Medication
description: Systolic blood pressure (mmHg) before and after medication for 15 patients.
studyDescription: Fifteen patients had their systolic blood pressure measured before and after starting an antihypertensive medication. Each patient serves as their own control in this paired before-after design. This is an observational study — patients were not randomly assigned to receive vs. not receive the medication.

variableDescriptions.before: Systolic blood pressure (mmHg) measured before the patient started taking the medication.
variableDescriptions.after: Systolic blood pressure (mmHg) measured after a period of taking the medication.

sourceDetail: Colton, T. (1974). Statistics in Medicine. Little, Brown. A widely used example for paired t-tests in biostatistics courses.

context.parameter: mean change in systolic blood pressure (after − before)
context.nullClaim: no change in blood pressure after medication
